Welcome back to a Sunday evening Wall Power. I=E2=80=99m Marion
Maneker.

=C2=A0

I hope you enjoyed Julie Davich=E2=80=99s takeover on Friday night, focused=
 on the most interesting gallery shows and exhibitions from Paris to New Yo=
rk. I received many kind notes of encouragement from readers asking for mor=
e gallery coverage. We=E2=80=99ll try to do more of those dedicated emails =
when and where we can. If you haven=E2=80=99t opened Friday=E2=80=99s email=
, look through your inbox now.
(I=E2=80=99ll wait...) If you have, and there=E2=80=99s something you think=
 we should be covering, or just want to share your thoughts, respond to thi=
s email or write Julie directly at JDavich@puck.news ( Jdavich@puck.news?ut=
m_campaign=3DWall+Power+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F26%2F25%29&utm_content=3DWall+=
Power+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F26%2F25%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=
=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ).

=C2=A0

Tonight, we=E2=80=99re back on the grind talking about Sotheby=E2=80=99s 20=
24 results call, and the surprising emphasis on their $1 billion real
estate portfolio.

=99d
added to his net worth over the course of the Biden administration. As many=
 of you will remember, the Blackstone C.E.O. had a christening party ( http=
s://puck.news/whats-behind-steve-schwarzmans-newport-mansion-museum/?utm_ca=
mpaign=3DWall+Power+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F26%2F25%29&utm_content=3DWall+Powe=
r+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%281%2F26%2F25%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcu=
stomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ) last summer for Miramar, his upda=
ted Newport cottage, which will become a private museum upon his death. The=
 gargantuan piles in Newport are not terribly distinguished as architectura=
l wonders or
treasure houses filled with art and antiques. That has made Schwarzman=E2=
=80=99s efforts to build a museum there=E2=80=94surely aided by teams of ex=
perts on loan from the Frick, where he is a significant patron=E2=80=94an e=
asier task.

=C2=A0

Now, the enthusiastic Trump supporter is turning his attention to something=
 a little harder: restoring Conholt Park, a house in the U.K., where the co=
mpetition is stiffer. He paid
=C2=A380 million in 2022 to buy the 17th century 2500-acre estate in Wiltsh=
ire. Gapper reports that Schwarzman has spent =C2=A325 million on a Sir Jos=
hua Reynolds portrait of Lady Worsley, which had been hanging in Harewood H=
ouse in West Yorkshire. (The Lascelles family sold because they needed mone=
y to keep up the house. A fortuitous call from Christie=E2=80=99s private s=
ales department proved too good to pass up.) At the same time, Schwarzman b=
ought a
Thomas Gainsborough portrait of Lady Bate-Dudley, painted in 1787, that had=
 been on loan to the Tate. The sale was brokered by Dickinson, according to=
 Gapper, who wasn=E2=80=99t able to ascertain the price.

=C2=A0

Schwarzman=E2=80=99s hunt for ancient paintings to assemble his ersatz line=
age isn=E2=80=99t over. Gapper reports that several owners of 18th century =
pictures have been approached. Since the
artworks are not meant to leave the United Kingdom, Gapper observes, the de=
almaking has been much easier.=C2=A0

=C2=A0

That=E2=80=99s enough for today. See you all on Tuesday,

n-bottom:0px;">I was a little surprised to see real estate get so much emph=
asis on a call
that seemed to be directed at rebuilding confidence in the company. But it =
does help explain a few things.</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;"=
>=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">For much of the past ye=
ar, the art industry=E2=80=94especially the art advisors, collectors, and f=
iduciaries who place estates at auction=E2=80=94has been focused on the fee=
 structure change that Sotheby=E2=80=99s announced last February. Indeed, t=
he sparse auction offerings last fall made it clear that the new fee
arrangement was hampering the company=E2=80=99s ability to compete. As we k=
now, the company did the right thing and reversed course.=C2=A0</p><p style=
=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bott=
om:0px;">The fee structure, however, didn=E2=80=99t cause the cash squeeze=
=E2=80=94though Sotheby=E2=80=99s management is still loath to acknowledge =
it, and C.E.O. <strong>Charlie Stewart </strong>insisted in the fall that t=
he ADQ investment was not a lifeline. Sotheby=E2=80=99s financial problems =
began even before
the new fees went into effect. The August report in the <em>FT</em> only co=
vered the company=E2=80=99s performance up until June. At the time, a compa=
ny representative told me that much of that 88 percent earnings plunge was =
due to one-time charges related to the real estate expansion and other thin=
gs.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=A0</p><p style=3D=
"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">I don=E2=80=99t know the cost of the new Pari=
s headquarters or the Breuer building in Manhattan, but they=E2=80=99re not
likely to come anywhere close to $1 billion. The new =E2=80=9Cmaison=E2=80=
=9D in Hong Kong is in a high-traffic luxury mall; it seems unlikely that S=
otheby=E2=80=99s owns much of that real estate. We do know that Sotheby=E2=
=80=99s still owns its current Manhattan headquarters on York Avenue=E2=80=
=94which it has rented to the Weill Cornell Medical Center, but at rates lo=
wer than what Sotheby=E2=80=99s itself was paying=E2=80=94as well as a ware=
house location in Queens.</p><p style=3D"margin:0;margin-bottom:0px;">=C2=
=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;
margin-bottom:0px;">On the call, Kownator emphasized that bond reports don=
=E2=80=99t include revenue from Sotheby=E2=80=99s Financial Services and th=
e real estate portfolio, though it isn=E2=80=99t clear that the latter is a=
 profit center. He closed by name-checking ADQ as a long-term partner, and =
citing the real estate=E2=80=99s =E2=80=9Cincreasing global footprint,=E2=
=80=9D the strength of their data, and the power of the Sotheby=E2=80=99s b=
rand as drivers of future growth.=C2=A0</p><p style=3D"margin:0;"><br/>Anyw=
ay, coming back to the intended
takeaway from the call: Sotheby=E2=80=99s wants to be seen as a global luxu=
ry retailer. This was <strong>Patrick Drahi</strong>=E2=80=99s goal from th=
e beginning. It appears he was willing to push the company to the brink to =
continue on that path. Now that Sotheby=E2=80=99s has the high-traffic Hong=
 Kong flagship and the elegant Paris position, and soon a New York landmark=
 in the Breuer building, we=E2=80=99ll see whether real estate can actually=
 have a transformative effect on its future.</p></div>
